title: B002: Implement Kubernetes-interacting packages
summary: Review #13 feedback from gndrmnn fully addressed: (1) database readiness checks refactored to use shared conditions.IsReady(), (2) entire normalize package deleted and replaced with unconditional updates (deployment, cronJob) and hash-based comparison (job), (3) GetTLSSecret returns (certPEM, keyPEM, err) instead of (*corev1.Secret, error), (4) EnsureService gained validateImmutableServiceFields guard for ClusterIP/ClusterIPs/IPFamilies, (5) typed_simulators.go merged into simulators.go. Net -686 lines. All 72 unit tests pass, go build/vet/golangci-lint clean, no dangling references to deleted normalize package.

tests_checklist.item: All unit tests pass (72/72 across deployment, job, tls, database)
Edge cases covered (no-hash-annotation backward compat, immutable field conflicts, matching immutable fields allowed)
Tests updated for GetTLSSecret new signature (certPEM, keyPEM, err)
Normalization-based tests properly removed alongside normalize package deletion
Hash annotation presence verified in Job creation and replacement tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (error wrapping with %w, controllerutil usage)
No dead code or unused imports remain
Consistent use of conditions.IsReady() across database package
Clean separation of concerns (createJobWithHash extracted as helper)

security_checklist.item: SHA-256 hashing used appropriately for PodSpec comparison
Fail-fast validation for immutable service fields prevents silent data corruption

architecture_checklist.item: Normalize package removal eliminates API-server-default mirroring maintenance burden
Hash-based Job comparison is simpler and more robust than normalization
GetTLSSecret returns data directly instead of leaking Secret object to callers

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Normalize package (9 files, ~500 lines) correctly identified as unnecessary and removed

fail_fast_checklist.item: validateImmutableServiceFields returns error immediately on conflict
GetTLSSecret validates presence of tls.crt and tls.key before returning
All API errors wrapped with context (namespace/name/operation)

defensive_checklist.item: createJobWithHash uses DeepCopy to avoid mutating caller's Job object
EnsureService works on DeepCopy of desired spec to avoid caller mutation
AlreadyExists handled gracefully in createJobWithHash (old Job still terminating)

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): deployment.go:144 — ipFamiliesEqual reimplements slices.Equal which is already imported and works with []corev1.IPFamily (IPFamily is a comparable type). Consider replacing with slices.Equal(a, b) to reduce code.

D2 (minor): job.go:31 — PodSpecHash discards the json.Marshal error with `data, _ := json.Marshal(spec)`. This is safe for *corev1.PodSpec (no unserializable types), but a brief comment explaining why would improve clarity for future readers.

detected_patterns.name: Hash-based spec comparison for immutable Kubernetes resources
detected_patterns.applies_when: Implementing create-or-update logic for Kubernetes resources whose specs are mutated by API-server defaulting (e.g., Jobs where PodSpec fields like ImagePullPolicy are filled in after creation), and you need to detect whether the desired spec has changed from what was originally created
detected_patterns.description: Instead of maintaining a normalization layer that mirrors API-server defaulting logic (fragile and requires updates with every Kubernetes version), a SHA-256 hash of the desired spec is stored as an annotation at creation time. On subsequent reconciliations, the hash of the current desired spec is compared against the stored annotation. This is used specifically for Jobs because they are immutable after creation — if the spec changes, the old Job must be deleted and a new one created. The annotation key follows the pattern 'forge.c5c3.io/<spec-type>-hash'.

detected_patterns.name: Unconditional spec update for mutable Kubernetes resources
detected_patterns.applies_when: Implementing create-or-update logic for mutable Kubernetes resources (Deployments, Services, CronJobs) where the API server applies defaulting that makes comparison unreliable without a normalization layer
detected_patterns.description: For mutable resources (Deployments, Services, CronJobs), the Ensure* functions always update the spec to the desired state on every reconciliation, rather than comparing existing vs desired specs. This avoids the maintenance burden of a normalization layer that mirrors API-server defaulting logic. The approach trades a small number of no-op API writes for complete elimination of false-positive and false-negative spec comparisons. Server-assigned immutable fields (ClusterIP, ClusterIPs, IPFamilies on Services) are explicitly preserved before the update.
